name: Chewy Caramel-Peach Squares with Pecans
recipeIngredient: 2 large Firm, ripe peaches, peeled and pitted, thinly sliced (about 2 1/2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, cream together the brown sugar, butter, and a generous dash of salt until smooth.
2. Gradually add the flour and mix until just combined. Set aside about half of this mixture for topping.
3. Refrigerate the batter until ready to use.
4. Spoon the remaining batter into a greased 9-inch square baking pan, spreading it evenly.
5. Press the dough down with plastic wrap or place in a small plastic bag and press gently.
6. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for about 15 minutes, or until lightly browned.
7. In a separate bowl, combine flour, sugar, salt, and cinnamon. Toss peach slices in this mixture.
8. Place the peach slices over the slightly cooled crust, covering it completely with all pieces.
9. Spread the reserved batter evenly over the peaches.
10. Beat the egg white until frothy and dab onto the topping.
11. Scatter pecans evenly over the top.
12. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for another 50 minutes, or until lightly browned.
13. Allow to cool completely on a wire rack before cutting into squares. Chill if desired.
14. Cut chilled pastry into quarters, then cut each quarter into 4 smaller squares.
Each serving contains approximately: 120 calories; 36 mg sodium; 8 mg cholesterol; 4 grams fat; 20 grams carbohydrates; 1 gram protein; 0.23 gram fiber.
